ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Aglio Olio e Peperoncino; closest parent 'pasta food product' (FOODON_00002473) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for aglio olio preparation exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific pasta preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Aglio Olio e Peperoncino is approximately 300 g, equivalent to roughly 200 g cooked pasta with olive oil, garlic, and chilli sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Pasta, cooked, enriched, without added salt — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 20121)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base pasta values derived from USDA SR Legacy cooked pasta (NDB 20121 / FDC 168936). Olive oil contribution estimated from USDA FoodData Central olive oil entry (FDC 171413) at approximately 15 g olive oil per 100 g finished dish. Garlic and chilli contribute negligible macros at typical quantities. Final per-100g values represent a weighted composite of cooked spaghetti (~75%) and olive oil (~15%) with minor garlic/chilli/parsley components (~10%), consistent with standard aglio olio preparation ratios.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 48 is based on cooked spaghetti (al dente) from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Aglio olio preparation does not substantially alter the GI of the pasta base; olive oil and garlic additions may slightly lower the effective GI. GL per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 x 85.5) / 100 ≈ 41.0, based on 300 g serving with 28.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.guidance: A 300 g restaurant serving provides approximately 540 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt preparation and monitor total daily sodium intake to remain within the 2000 mg/day recommended limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic load and refined carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low GI of 48 but a high glycemic load of approximately 41 per restaurant serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 150–200 g and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Olive oil quality and saturated fat profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The predominance of monounsaturated fat from extra-virgin olive oil (approximately 6.8 g per 100 g) is cardioprotective; individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume moderate portions as part of a Mediterranean-style dietary pattern, provided total energy intake is managed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Aglio Olio e Peperoncino is a classic Italian pasta dish originating from the Lazio region of Italy, particularly associated with Roman cuisine. The name translates literally as 'garlic, oil, and chilli pepper'. The dish is prepared by sautéing thinly sliced or minced garlic and dried red chilli flakes in generous quantities of extra-virgin olive oil until the garlic is golden and fragrant, then tossing the mixture with al dente cooked spaghetti and finishing with fresh flat-leaf parsley. Some restaurant versions, including the field survey data listing from Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore, incorporate cherry tomatoes for added acidity and sweetness. The texture is silky and lightly coated rather than saucy, with a bold garlic and chilli heat balanced by the fruity richness of olive oil. Nutritionally, the dish is moderate in energy (approximately 220 kcal per 100 g), relatively low in saturated fat, and provides a meaningful source of monounsaturated fatty acids from olive oil, which are associated with cardiovascular benefit. The glycemic index is low (GI 48) due to the al dente pasta preparation, though the glycemic load per restaurant serving is high owing to portion size. The dish is naturally vegan, dairy-free, and halal-suitable. It is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its moderate-to-high glycemic load at typical restaurant portions, and for hypertensive individuals due to restaurant-level sodium additions.

confidence_reason: No exact match for Aglio Olio e Peperoncino found in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are derived from USDA SR Legacy cooked pasta and USDA olive oil data with weighted composite estimation. GI is based on analogous spaghetti data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient composition is well-defined and the USDA source data is high quality.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite: approximately 75% cooked spaghetti (USDA FDC 168936), 15% olive oil (USDA FDC 171413), and 10% garlic, chilli, parsley components. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as olive oil and pasta contain no industrially produced trans fats (naturally zero). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as no vitamin D-containing ingredients are present. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as all ingredients are plant-derived. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value 6.8 estimated from olive oil contribution). All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the USDA SR Legacy pasta entry and not calculable from the composite without additional micronutrient data for olive oil fractions; however all such fields have been populated with estimates where defensible. GI of 48 is based on spaghetti al dente from University of Sydney GI Database; aglio olio preparation does not materially alter pasta GI. Restaurant version at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore (Boat Quay, Singapore) priced at SGD 23.98 per serving. The description in the input mentions cherry tomato which is incorporated into the ingredient list and description.
